agent_raw:
The benchmark has four interrelated flaws that prevent a clean superiority claim.

Objective drift: The incumbent operates under its original renewal-probability objective. The challenger is evaluated on net contribution margin. Comparing across objectives does not establish which policy is better on a shared metric.

Holdout contamination: DR nuisance models were fit on all four quarters including Q3-Q4. The reporting window is Q3-Q4. The evaluator uses data from the period it is supposed to evaluate blindly.

Threshold on reporting slice: The operating cutoff was chosen to maximize margin on Q3-Q4. Reporting margin performance at the threshold selected on the same Q3-Q4 data is circular.

Asymmetric reoptimization: The challenger received fresh Bayesian tuning; the incumbent was left on its original 18-month calibration. The comparison penalizes the incumbent for calibration age rather than comparing current-best versions of both policies.

The bench mark needs to be rebuilt with: (1) a shared objective metric for both policies; (2) nuisance models fit on pre-reporting data only; (3) threshold selection on a pre-reporting validation set; (4) updated incumbent calibration. Leadership should not approve now.
all_issues_raised: Objective changed between incumbent (renewal probability) and challenger (net contribution margin)
DR nuisance models fit on full experiment log including Q3-Q4 reporting period
Operating cutoff selected on same Q3-Q4 cohort used for headline reporting
Asymmetric optimization: challenger received Bayesian tuning, incumbent on 18-month-old calibration

empirical_test.condition: Refit nuisance models excluding reporting slice, select cutoff on pre-reporting validation set, re-calibrate incumbent to same margin objective, evaluate both on frozen Q3-Q4 holdout
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Challenger profit lift falls below zero or CI includes zero under clean holdout protocol on shared metric
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Challenger beats re-optimized incumbent by at least 3% incremental profit with block-aware 95% CI excluding zero
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Positive but sub-3% lift, or heterogeneous gains across regions with some negative
